data.title: SpaceX Enters Pentagon's Contest to Build Voice-Controlled Military Drone Swarms
data.description:
Elon Musk's aerospace company, SpaceX, along with its subsidiary xAI, is quietly competing in a new Pentagon contest that's focused on developing voice-controlled, autonomous drone-swarming technology. The program, which offers a $100 million prize, wants to develop systems that can translate spoken commands into digital instructions and coordinate multiple drones at once. The six-month competition is being run jointly by the Defense Innovation Unit and the Defense Autonomous Warfare Group, which is a new branch within the U.S. Special Operations Command.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The goal is to create drone swarms that can operate in both the air and the sea, while also being able to share target information and potentially carry out missions from launch to finish. While flying multiple drones simultaneously is already possible, building software that allows them to move autonomously as a coordinated swarm remains a major technical challenge. At the same time, the project raises ethical and operational concerns.

For example, generative AI tools like large language models are known to produce biased or inaccurate outputs, which has made some defense officials worried about giving them too much authority in combat settings. In addition, although SpaceX is already a major defense contractor, its entry into offensive AI-enabled weapons development is a notable change for Musk. Indeed, he has previously warned against designing weapons that can kill without meaningful human control.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

When it comes to Elon Musk's companies, most of them are privately held. However, retail investors can invest in his most popular company, Tesla (TSLA). Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $396.80 per share implies 5% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Polymarket Odds: Will PANW, CZR, SUN Beat Earnings Tomorrow?
data.description:
On Tuesday, February 17, Palo Alto Networks (PANW), Caesars Entertainment (CZR), and Sunoco (SUN) will report their earnings for the period ended December 31. Sunoco will report before the market open, while Palo Alto and Caesars' results will come after the closing bell. Traders are already weighing in with their expectations on Polymarket.

  • Palo Alto Networks is expected to report an adjusted EPS of 94 cents.
  • Caesars Entertainment is expected to report a GAAP EPS loss of 18 cents.
  • Sunoco is expected to report a GAAP EPS of $1.62.

What is Polymarket?

Polymarket is a prediction platform where traders bet on the outcomes of real-world events, including earnings reports, elections, and economic indicators. Polymarket odds reflect expectations and can provide insight into investor sentiment.

Will Palo Alto Networks Beat Earnings?

Palo Alto Networks has beaten or met earnings during 18 of the past 18 quarters for a success rate of 100%. Polymarket gives the cybersecurity company an 87% chance of beating its estimated adjusted EPS of 94 cents.

Will Caesars Entertainment Beat Earnings?

Caesars Entertainment has beaten or met earnings during 5 of the past 18 quarters for a success rate of 27.77%. Polymarket assigns a 27% chance that the hotel and casino company will top its estimated GAAP EPS loss of 18 cents this quarter.

Will Sunoco Beat Earnings?

Sunoco has beaten or met earnings during 8 of the past 18 quarters for a success rate of 44.44%. Polymarket traders give the fuel distributor a 16% chance of surpassing its estimated GAAP EPS of $1.62.

Disclosure: Polymarket odds reflect expectations, not guaranteed outcomes. The odds represent the views and expectations of traders, but actual earnings results can differ significantly from these predictions. Investors should treat Polymarket data as just one tool when evaluating their investment decisions.

data.title: Should Investors Buy Palantir Stock (PLTR) after the Recent Pullback? Wall Street Weighs in
data.description:
Palantir Technologies (PLTR) stock has plunged about 27% over the past month despite the company's stellar Q4 2025 results. Broader concerns about the impact of artificial intelligence (AI) models on enterprise software companies, fears of an AI bubble, and bearish comments from Michael Burry, founder and former CEO of the now dissolved Scion Asset Management, dragged down PLTR stock. Nonetheless, Wall Street is cautiously optimistic on the data analytics company, with the average price target indicating solid upside potential from current levels.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Analysts' Views on PLTR Stock

On Monday, PhillipCapital analyst Paul Chew reiterated a Buy rating on Palantir stock but lowered his price target to $190 from $208. The 5-star analyst noted that the company's impressive Q4 2025 performance was driven by robust momentum in its U.S. Commercial segment and continued strength in the U.S. Government contract execution and new awards. Chew added that Palantir expects Q1 2026 revenue to grow about 74% year-over-year to $1.53 billion, supported by larger deal sizes and accelerating customer conversions from AIP (Artificial Intelligence Platform) bootcamps, especially in the U.S. Commercial business.

The analyst adjusted his Fiscal 2026 estimates and lowered the terminal growth rate in his model to 7.5% from 8% to reflect normalized long-term revenue growth and intensifying competition from other AI analytics providers. "We remain positive on Palantir as its Ontology moat and AIP tools drive faster enterprise AI adoption beyond defence to other industries (Commercial revenue: +73% YoY in FY26e)," said Chew.

Meanwhile, D.A. Davidson analyst Gil Luria reiterated a Hold rating on PLTR stock and stated that he "read all 10,000 words in Michael Burry's newsletter and found no new reason to worry about Palantir." Luria added that while he appreciates Burry and finds his criticism of the AI cycle thoughtful, he believes the Big Short investor's latest report on Palantir does not provide any new evidence or argument that would make him change his opinion. While Luria has a "positive view" of Palantir, he is neutral on the stock due to its lofty valuation.

Is PLTR a Good Stock to Buy?

Overall, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Palantir Technologies stock based on 11 Buys, six Holds, and two Sell recommendations. The average PLTR stock price target of $191.25 indicates 45.5% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: CDS Markets Heat Up as AI Spending Boom Sparks a Surge in Big Tech Credit Hedging
data.description:
Debt investors are becoming increasingly worried that tech companies may borrow too aggressively in their race to dominate artificial intelligence. As AI spending accelerates, many hyperscalers are turning to debt markets to fund massive infrastructure builds. Indeed, industrywide spending is expected to exceed $3 trillion, much of it funded with borrowing. In addition, Morgan Stanley (MS) estimates that hyperscaler borrowing could hit $400 billion this year, up from $165 billion in 2025. As a result, activity in the credit default swaps (CDS) market has surged.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, credit default swaps allow investors and banks to hedge against debt risk. Interestingly, according to data from the Depository Trust & Clearing Corp., single-name credit default swaps on high-grade big-tech issuers were barely active a year ago but are now among the most-traded contracts outside the financial sector. More specifically, activity has surged in names like Oracle (ORCL), Meta Platforms (META), and Alphabet (GOOGL), with roughly $895 million in Alphabet-related CDS and about $687 million in Meta-related CDS currently outstanding.

Meanwhile, banks that underwrite these massive financing deals are also using CDS markets to hedge their own balance sheets. In fact, trading desks are now quoting $20 million to $50 million blocks in CDS contracts that barely traded a year ago, and some firms have even created baskets of hyperscaler credit default swaps similar to bond baskets. Nevertheless, some hedge funds argue that trillion-dollar companies with strong balance sheets are likely to outperform in most stress scenarios, making it attractive to sell protection.

Which Tech Stock Is the Better Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, out of the three stocks mentioned above, analysts think that ORCL stock has the most room to run. In fact, ORCL's average price target of $289.62 per share implies almost 81% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Hedge Funds Post Largest Purchases of Asian Stocks in 10 Years
data.description:
Last week, hedge funds recorded their largest net buys of emerging and developed Asian stocks since 2016, the year that Goldman Sachs' Prime Services trading desk began tracking the metric.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Goldman added that long buys outpaced short covers by a ratio of 8.4 to 1, signaling that buys weren't driven by short sellers getting squeezed but instead by outright purchases. Many emerging markets have outperformed the S&P 500 (SPX) this year amid a weakening dollar, led by a 31% return from the iShares MSCI South Korea ETF (EWY).

Hedge Funds Turn to Asian Stocks, Reduce U.S. Real Estate Exposure

Semiconductor and memory leaders have driven South Korea's performance, with Samsung Electronics and (SMSN) SK Hynix both up by more than 30% year-to-date. Meanwhile, the iShares MSCI Emerging Markets ETF (EEM) has returned 8.7% this year compared to the S&P 500's loss of 0.3%.

While hedge funds have shifted into Asian markets, they've also been selling off real estate stocks. Last week, hedge funds sold stocks in the U.S. real estate sector at the fastest pace since September 2022 and for a third consecutive week.

data.title: Harvard University Opens New Position in Ethereum (ETH)
data.description:
Harvard is buying the dip in cryptocurrencies.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The endowment of the highly regarded university has opened a new position in Ethereum (ETH). A filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) shows that Harvard's $56.9 billion endowment made its first foray into Ethereum in the fourth quarter of 2025.

The Harvard endowment bought 3.9 million shares of BlackRock's (BLK) iShares Ethereum Trust (ETHA), valued at about $87 million. The new position was opened as the price of ETH and other cryptocurrencies declined sharply since mid-October of last year.

Harvard Sells Bitcoin

The latest SEC filing shows that Harvard's endowment also boosted investments in chipmakers Broadcom (AVGO) and TSMC (TSM), as well as Google's parent company Alphabet (GOOGL) and railroad operator Union Pacific (UNP) during the final months of 2025.

Harvard trimmed its existing stakes in Amazon (AMZN), Microsoft (MSFT), and Nvidia (NVDA). It also cut its exposure to Bitcoin (BTC) during Q4 2025. The endowment lowered its stake in the iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) by 21%, selling 1.5 million shares. However, the Bitcoin exchange-traded fund (ETF) remains Harvard's largest holding at $265.8 million.

Some students and market observers have criticized Harvard's endowment fund for holding volatile Bitcoin as its largest position. Currently trading at $68,000, Bitcoin's price has been nearly cut in half from an all-time high of $126,000 reached last October.

Is Bitcoin a Buy?

Most analysts don't offer ratings or price targets on Bitcoin. So instead, we'll look at the three-month performance of BTC. As one can see in the chart below, the price of Bitcoin has declined 25% in the last 12 weeks.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: AI Disruption Worries Are Spreading to European Stocks, Warns Morgan Stanley
data.description:
Concerns about artificial intelligence disrupting stock markets are no longer limited to the United States. While European equities have performed slightly better than U.S. markets in recent weeks, anxiety is beginning to build there as well. In fact, according to Morgan Stanley (MS), investors are now assigning AI disruption risk to about 24% of the MSCI Europe index's (IEUR) weight, compared with just 4% a month ago. In other words, the perceived impact of AI on European companies has risen sharply in a short period of time.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

What initially started as worries about slower software revenue growth has now evolved into concerns about long-term disruption across multiple industries. Morgan Stanley analysts even noted that there are similarities to how market psychology shifted during the early stages of the COVID-19 pandemic, though they emphasized that this is not their base case. Nevertheless, they compared the situation to early 2020, when concerns about China first hit travel-related stocks, then spread to industrials and banks, and eventually led to a widespread global selloff.

As a result, the bank believes it is worth examining a downside scenario in which disruption fears continue to ripple through additional sectors. That said, not all industries appear to be equally vulnerable. For example, utilities, semiconductors, defense, and tobacco are viewed as more resilient sectors if AI worries spread further. On the other hand, software, travel, media, and banks are considered to be more exposed. Still, Morgan Stanley maintained a Buy rating on European banks and expects European equities overall to continue outperforming U.S. stocks.

Is IEUR Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on IEUR stock based on 584 Buys, 322 Holds, and 57 Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average IEUR price target of $82.47 per share implies 9.7% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Gen Z Turns to the Stock Market Instead of Home Ownership
data.description:
Amid record-high home prices and mortgage rates stubbornly remaining above 6%, Generation Z, or people born between 1997 and 2012, have parked their money in the stock market instead of real estate.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Between 2013 and 2023, the share of 25- to 39-year-olds contributing annually to investment accounts more than tripled to 14.4%, according to the JPMorgan Chase Institute. Meanwhile, baby boomers, born between 1946 and 1964, account for 42% of all homebuyers.

"We've seen really strong, surprisingly strong growth in retail investing in recent years among people who may otherwise be first-time home buyers," said JPMorgan Chase Research Director George Eckerd.

Gen Z Embraces Investing as Housing Grows Unaffordable

Eckerd added that the stock market rally and easy-to-use trading platforms have further encouraged Gen Z to invest in stocks. Social media is also a likely driver behind the trend, with investing communities emerging on X and Reddit (RDDT).

That contrasts with the mortgage experience, which remains far more complex, costly, and out of reach for many Gen Z households. In 1999, Americans between the ages of 18 and 39 accounted for 51% of home buyers. That figure dropped to 44% last year as homes remain unaffordable for many prospective buyers.

data.title: PPLT – An ETF to Take Advantage of Powerful Platinum Prices
data.description:
The Platinum Spot US Dollar price has rocketed 110% over the last 12 months to around $2,051 an ounce. That's down to a host of drivers from lack of supply, Chinese demand, the growth of hydrogen fuel-cell and electrolyzer technologies and catalytic converters in hybrid vehicles.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The launch of physically backed platinum and palladium futures contracts by China's Guangzhou Futures Exchange (GFEX) last year has also helped boost demand.

According to analysts, the price could keep rising this year to near $2,500 an ounce.

One of the best ways to take advantage of the growth in precious platinum is via an ETF. Let's look at the Aberdeen Standard Physical Platinum Shares ETF (PPLT), one of TipRanks Best Commodities ETFs.

PPLT

As a physically held ETF, PPLT provides direct exposure to platinum by holding physical platinum bullion, ensuring that the fund's value closely mirrors the fluctuations in platinum prices. This ETF is tailored for those looking to diversify their portfolios with a commodity that is not only crucial in various industrial applications, including automotive and electronics, but also esteemed in jewelry and investment sectors.

With PPLT, investors can tap into the potential growth of platinum's market value driven by factors such as technological advancements, environmental regulations, and geopolitical influences that impact the supply and demand of this lustrous metal. It tracks the Platinum London PM Fix ($/ozt) index.
data.author: David Craik
